title: 11 Herbal Creams For Chills
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to relieve chills, especially when combined with other natural remedies like teas. When you drink herbal teas, the heat from the liquid helps to warm you up from the inside out. This is because the hot liquid increases blood flow and circulation, which can help to reduce the chill feeling.  Herbal teas work by releasing the active compounds from the herbs, such as Echinacea purpurea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to fight off infections. Echinacea can help to boost your immune system, making it easier to shake off chills and recover from illness. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, is another herb that can help to relieve chills. It contains compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it easier to warm up.  Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus, is another herb that can help to relieve chills. It contains compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. When you drink eucalyptus tea, the heat from the liquid can help to warm you up and relieve congestion.  Using herbal creams in combination with herbal teas can be a great way to relieve chills and stay healthy. The creams can provide a topical application of the herbs, helping to warm up the skin and muscles, while the teas can help to warm up the body from the inside out.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help alleviate chills. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, while caffeic acid derivatives have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation. Flavonoids, such as quercetin, also have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate chills. By reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system, Echinacea purpurea creams may provide relief from chills by addressing their underlying causes.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted mixture and steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Add shea butter and vitamin E oil to the bowl and mix well until combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Echinacea purpurea cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve pain and reduce swelling. The warming properties of Zingiber officinale creams also help to increase blood flow to the affected area, which can help to reduce chills and fever. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of gingerol and shogaol can help to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of coldness and discomfort. By applying Zingiber officinale creams topically, the active constituents can help to provide relief from chills by addressing the underlying causes of the symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale essential oil and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the melted mixture.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in the shea butter until it's fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before applying it to relieve chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains eucalyptol, a key bioactive constituent that helps relieve chills by opening up airways and relieving congestion. Eucalyptol's decongestant properties make it easier to breathe, reducing the discomfort associated with chills. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to eucalyptol and other compounds like alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, also help soothe the skin and reduce fever. Eucalyptus globulus creams can be applied topically to help bring down body temperature and provide temporary relief from chills. The cooling sensation from eucalyptol and other compounds in the cream can also help calm the body and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 10 drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave (30 seconds at a time).
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and add Eucalyptus globulus essential oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use. Store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids as its active constituents. These compounds help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. This is especially beneficial for people who experience chills due to poor circulation or inflammation. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba creams also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. By improving circulation and reducing inflammation, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to alleviate chills and promote a sense of warmth in the body.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1/4 cup of Ginkgo biloba extract, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract to the melted mixture and stir well.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. Then, whip it with a hand mixer or a whisk until it becomes light and fluffy.
Transfer the mixture to a container and store it in the refrigerator. Apply as needed to soothe chills and cold skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Glycyrrhizin also acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the body, which can help combat infections that cause chills. The anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams help soothe and calm the body, reducing the discomfort associated with chills. By reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of mucus, these creams can help provide relief from chills.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of glycerin, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in a pot of boiling water.
In a separate bowl, mix distilled water, glycerin, Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and aloe vera gel.
Slowly add the melted beeswax to the water mixture and stir until combined. Add vitamin E oil and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before using as a cream for chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid, isovaleramide, and valepotriates as its active constituents. These compounds have a sedative and relaxing effect on the body, which helps to calm the nervous system. The valepotriates in Valeriana officinalis creams work by reducing the activity of neurotransmitters that stimulate the brain, leading to a decrease in body temperature and the sensation of chills. Additionally, valerenic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body, further contributing to its ability to alleviate chills. By reducing the body's internal heat and calming the nervous system, Valeriana officinalis creams can help to provide relief from chills and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis root and 2 cups of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the coconut oil to 160°F (71°C) and add the dried root. Let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the coconut oil mixture. Melt over low heat.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Use as a cream for chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains active constituents like thujone, rosmarinic acid, and camphor. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce discomfort and relieve chills. Thujone, in particular, has a cooling effect on the body, helping to lower fever and alleviate cold sweats. Rosmarinic acid also has a soothing effect on the skin, reducing redness and promoting relaxation. By combining these properties, Salvia officinalis creams can help provide relief from chills and promote a sense of comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves. Measure 1/4 cup of the leaves and put them in a bowl.
Add 2 cups of water to the bowl and let it steep for 10 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another bowl.
Measure 1/2 cup of coconut oil or shea butter and add it to the strained liquid. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or in a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil. Stir until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely. Store the Salvia officinalis creams in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These components have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with chills. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus alleviating discomfort. The flavonoids present in Achillea millefolium creams possess antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Achillea millefolium creams can provide relief from chills and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.

Infuse the coconut oil with Achillea millefolium flowers by placing the flowers in a clean glass jar, adding the coconut oil, and letting it sit in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the infused coconut oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
In a small saucepan, melt the beeswax and shea butter over low heat, then add the strained coconut oil and vitamin E oil, stirring until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Achillea millefolium cream is ready to use as a natural remedy for chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains high levels of linalool and linalyl acetate, two active constituents known for their antispasmodic and antihistaminic properties. These compounds help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective in relieving chills and fever. The antihistaminic properties of linalool also help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chills. Additionally, the calming effects of linalyl acetate can help to soothe the body and promote relaxation, further reducing the sensation of chills. By applying Lavandula angustifolia creams to the skin, these active constituents can be absorbed and help to alleviate chills and fever.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water to 120°F (49°C).
Add the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the strained coconut oil mixture. Heat the mixture to 140°F (60°C) to melt the wax and butter.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool. The cream will thicken as it cools. Use 1-2 tablespoons as needed to soothe chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils with active constituents like rosmarinic acid, camphor, and borneol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to ease muscle tension and relieve chills. The camphor in Rosmarinus officinalis creams has a cooling effect on the skin, reducing the sensation of chills and discomfort. The borneol in the cream helps to improve blood circulation, promoting warmth and relaxation in the body. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams to the skin, you can benefit from its soothing and warming properties, which can help to alleviate chills and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in the saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycerin to the saucepan. Stir until the mixture is well combined.
Add 20-25 drops of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a bowl and let it cool until it thickens. Stir occasionally until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active compounds that have antimicrobial and antiviral properties. These compounds help to reduce the severity of chills by fighting off the underlying infection that's causing them. The antiviral properties of thymol and carvacrol also help to boost the body's immune system, making it easier to shake off the infection. Additionally, thymol has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with chills. By using Thymus vulgaris creams, you can help to alleviate the symptoms of chills and support your body's natural healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan until it reaches 160°F (71°C).
In a separate saucepan, melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Combine the heated water and melted oil mixture, then add the dried Thymus vulgaris leaves. Remove from heat and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, then let cool and thicken before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chills from forming. Certain herbs like ginger and peppermint contain properties that can help warm the body from the inside. When you feel a chill coming on, try sipping on a hot cup of herbal tea to see if it makes a difference. It might just do the trick.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm chills, consuming them every day might not be the best idea. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can cause stomach issues if had excessively. Others, like chamomile, might make you drowsy if taken in large amounts, so moderation is key.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with chills, but the time it takes to feel better varies. Some people notice a difference in 30 minutes to an hour after drinking a warm tea. Others might feel better within 2-3 hours. It's different for everyone, and it depends on the tea and the severity of the chills.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for chills is most effective when your body's core temperature is dropping. This usually happens during the late afternoon or evening hours. So, if you're feeling chilly, brew a cup of herbal tea around 4 or 5 pm, and let its warmth soothe you before bed.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for chills. Some herbs, like ginseng and feverfew, can affect blood pressure or slow blood clotting. This means they might make medications stronger or increase the risk of side effects. It's best to talk to the person taking the medications about any herbal teas they're consuming.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and feeling chilly, herbal teas might sound like a soothing solution. However, some herbs can be risky, like pennyroyal and sage, which can cause uterus contractions. Others, like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger, are usually considered safe, but still, it's best to limit your intake and check the tea's ingredients.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to ease chills, but it's essential to check the ingredients. Some herbs, like sage and peppermint, are generally safe for breastfeeding. However, others like pennyroyal and wormwood can be problematic. If you're concerned, choose teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding.
title_2: Chills Got You Down, Older Adults? Try 11 Herbal Creams
